summary.brief: Estate of Athy v. Edgewood. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Terry, who had dementia, became a resident of the Edgewood Kalispell Memory Care facility (Edgewood) in June 2021.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Terry passed away on November 23, 2021. Just shy of two years later, on November 20, 2023, Athy, individually and as personal representative for his mother’s estate, filed a complaint against Edgewood.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Athy alleged that Terry was in a soiled diaper for days or weeks prior to hospitalization and that she died shortly after from infections and complications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We hold that Counts I-VI of Athy’s “First Amended Complaint” (FAC) were time-barred.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We hold that the District Court correctly construed Counts I-VI of Athy’s FAC as medical malpractice claims subject to §§ 27-2-205(1) and 25-3-106, MCA, time limits and service of process requirements.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: However, we hold that Athy’s unjust enrichment and contract rescission claims were not medical malpractice claims and not subject to §§ 27-2-205(1) or 25-3-106, MCA.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The FAC was thus a new complaint, not an amended one, and was time-barred by § 27-2-205(1), MCA.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Abdominal pain, blood in stool, incontinence, and a large, soiled rash
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was in a soiled diaper for days or weeks prior to hospitalization
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Possible infection due to prolonged exposure to soiled conditions

requestables.name: Complete Blood Count
requestables.description: To assess for signs of infection

requestables.name: Abdominal Imaging
requestables.description: To assess for any internal complications

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with abdominal pain and signs of infection

key_events.event: Patient taken to hospital for treatment

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Workup
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and initial management of patient's condition

key_events.event: Assessment of abdominal pain and rash

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in memory care facility with signs of infection and poor hygiene
decision_points.prompt: What should the facility staff have done upon noticing the patient's condition?

options.description: Regularly check and change the patient's diaper and monitor skin condition
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for preventing infections and skin breakdown

options.description: Delay changing the diaper and monitoring the skin condition
options.clinical_reasoning: Neglectful and increases risk of infection

actual_action_defendant.description: Facility staff failed to regularly check and change the patient's diaper

expected_action_court.description: Regular checks and changes should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence on standard care for memory care facilities

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide basic hygiene care led to infection
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Regular monitoring and care to prevent complications
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from infections and complications

key_findings: Failure to serve complaint within statutory period

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to serve complaint in time

factual_timeline.event: Terry became a resident of Edgewood Kalispell Memory Care

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide adequate care and hygiene

court_findings.legal_significance: Dismissal due to statute of limitations
